{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current gains came from fitting the fixed 256-step budget better, so the next likely bottleneck is model capacity. A moderate width increase plus one more residual block should improve feature quality without introducing architectural complexity or optimizer instability.","secondary_modes":["caching","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            nn.Linear(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, FC_HIDDEN),","old":"            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model is still essentially single-scale. Introducing one stride-2 stage and expanding channels in the lower-resolution stage should improve feature hierarchy and receptive field while keeping compute controlled, which is a strong fit for the fixed-step CIFAR-10 budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The two-stage hierarchy helped immediately, so the next cheap capacity increase is to deepen only the lower-resolution stage where extra residual depth costs less compute. That should improve representation quality without the larger runtime jump that widening the whole network again would cause.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),

            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels * CHANNEL_MULT, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels * CHANNEL_MULT) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),

            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels * CHANNEL_MULT, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current layout looks strong, so the next efficient lever is gradient dynamics. AdamW with slightly lighter decoupled decay and a lower second-moment timescale should adapt faster within only 256 steps while keeping regularization in place.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The AdamW/beta change worsened verified loss, so the best next move is to restore the previously stronger optimizer settings before trying a different axis. This removes complexity that did not pay for itself and returns to the best visible optimization behavior.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current two-stage design is strong, and the cheapest way to test more capacity is to widen only the lower-resolution stage. Increasing `CHANNEL_MULT` raises representational power where compute is relatively cheaper, without adding extra residual depth or changing the training loop.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The widened second stage improved loss, so a small increase in classifier capacity is a cheap next test. Expanding the hidden layer strengthens the readout head with minimal runtime cost compared with widening earlier convolutional stages again.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger classifier head increased complexity and worsened verified loss, so the next step is to restore the leaner readout that matched the best visible result. This keeps the stronger widened backbone while removing a change that did not justify itself.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With the backbone now stronger, the current label smoothing may be slightly over-regularizing this no-noise task. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Reducing label smoothing helped on this clean-label workload, which suggests the model still benefits from less regularization. {"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Removing label smoothing regressed, so the best next move is to restore the milder `0.05` setting that produced the strongest visible result. {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size lets the model see substantially more training examples while keeping the optimizer trajectory simple. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The large batch increase produced the strongest gain so far, which often shifts the best learning-rate scale upward. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The higher learning rate paired with the larger batch delivered another substantial gain, so the next efficient test is to continue scaling it upward modestly. {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation loss has continued improving as learning rate increased under the large-batch regime, so the next most efficient probe is another modest step upward. `2.5e-3` keeps the same stable setup while testing whether the current schedule still benefits from a larger optimization scale.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss has improved consistently as the learning-rate scale increased in the current large-batch configuration, so the highest-value next probe is another modest step upward. `3e-3` preserves the now-strong setup and tests whether optimization is still underpowered rather than changing multiple variables at once.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The jump to `3e-3` slightly worsened verified loss, so the best next move is to restore `2.5e-3`, which is the strongest visible learning-rate setting in the current large-batch regime.
